form.label: Scenario Control
form.description: Changing the scenario reloads the saved activation, fault start, and fault duration from local/ai_lab_scenarios.conf (read-only status). Submit applies your edits: Enable sets <scenario>_activated to the current epoch time; Disable sets it to 0.

search.query: | workshopregion action="status" | eval region_lower=lower(coalesce(region, "")) | table region_lower

html.p: Fault Start (minutes): Minutes after scenario activation (the saved <scenario>_activated epoch) before the simulated fault begins. Stored as <scenario>_fault_start in local/ai_lab_scenarios.conf. Use 0 to start the fault window at activation time.

Fault Duration (minutes): How long the fault window stays active after it starts. Stored as <scenario>_fault_duration. Use 0 to keep the fault in effect until the scenario is deactivated (lab default behavior).

search.query: | scenariocontrol action=set scenario="$scenario$" active="$active$" fault_start="$fault_start$" fault_duration="$fault_duration$" | eval activated_local_time = strftime(activated, "%F %T %Z") | fields status message scenario active activated_local_time fault_start fault_duration
